Secret scoping (the core value proposition of GitHub Environments) is handled by Vault RBAC policies at the infrastructure/staging-* and infrastructure/prod-* path level — no GitHub Secrets namespace is used for environment-specific secrets. The only remaining use case (required-reviewer gate for prod-deploy.yml) is a sub-task of ENV-05-T1 and will be implemented there if needed. Creating empty GitHub Environments at this stage would add UI noise without security benefit given the Vault architecture.

After all 6 repos are committed in Gate 8C, the implementing agent MUST verify cross-repo integration before closing the session: (1) open a test PR in the smallest repo (match-backend) and verify the self-hosted runner picks up the job; (2) confirm the composite action (CICD-09-T1, hosted in supabase-receptor) resolves from the calling repo; (3) verify branch-namespace naming (ARCH-01) does not collide when 2 repos both have a branch with the same name. Per-repo verification gates (npm build, pytest, etc.) are necessary but not sufficient for Gate 8C.

For Phases 7.5–12, the implementing agent must verify the following before declaring a phase complete: (1) All phase tasks marked complete in recommendations.json; (2) All repos committed and pushed to audit/260312-cicd-environments; (3) Per-repo verification gate passed for every changed repo; (4) Cross-repo verification passed if phase involves >1 repo; (5) recommendations.md re-rendered; (6) audit-brief.json sessionHistory entry added; (7) audit-brief.json openTaskCount updated; (8) SoA controls updated if phase has compliance tasks; (9) No new lint/type/build regressions introduced.

clarifications.item: ARCH-03-T0 parallel trial repo scope (comprehensive_review_3 ENH-2)
clarifications.decision: The 7-run parallel trial (ARCH-03-T0) should use 2 representative repos — preference-frontend (most complex frontend CI) and match-backend (simplest backend CI). Both must achieve 7 consecutive clean runs on the self-hosted runner with ubuntu-latest as a commented fallback. Only after this 2-repo trial succeeds should the remaining 4 repos be migrated to the self-hosted runner in a single batch.

implementationPhases.name: PLAN — Architecture Design, ADRs & Pre-Implementation Documents
implementationPhases.rationale: All planning documents that must exist before any system is provisioned. No infrastructure changes. Produces: ADR-001 to ADR-004 (k3s, self-hosted runner, isolation strategy, Docker image pinning), cluster runbook template (DOC-03), CI troubleshooting (DOC-04), DR plan (DOC-05), required check matrix (PROC-01 — moved here from Phase 11 as it gates Phase 12 branch protection), staging-to-prod promotion runbook (ENV-09), and Windows host reboot recovery checklist (OPS-01). These documents gate Phase 3 implementation.

implementationPhases.name: PLAN — Compliance Scope Review, Helmfile Design & Required Check Matrix
implementationPhases.rationale: Design tasks with no running system dependency: design and commit helmfile.yaml structure with pinned chart versions and quarterly upgrade workflow (ARCH-09); document the required CI status check matrix per repo (PROC-01) which is a pre-requisite for Phase 12 branch protection and must be written before any infrastructure changes are made. Both outputs are consumed by Phase 3 and Phase 12.

implementationPhases.name: IMPLEMENT — Core Cluster Stack (k3s, Vault, Calico, RBAC, Grafana, cert-manager, Falco, Tunnel, Slack)
implementationPhases.rationale: The strategic Phase 1 buildout. Provision k3s on Hyper-V, deploy all cluster services via helmfile.yaml: Calico CNI, Vault OSS (YubiKey PKCS#11 primary + TPM 2.0 secondary unseal), kube-prometheus-stack, Loki, Falco, cert-manager (wildcard TLS via DNS-01), Traefik ingress. Configure RBAC ServiceAccounts and RoleBindings for all workloads (SEC-08 — must be done at provisioning time, not retrofitted). Provision Cloudflare Tunnel for staging. Bind Supabase Studio to 127.0.0.1. Configure Alertmanager → Slack webhook pipeline. This phase has the most risk and longest duration — all subsequent phases depend on it.

implementationPhases.name: IMPLEMENT — Critical CI Auth Fixes (parallel-safe with Phase 3 planning)
implementationPhases.rationale: Fix the two security-class CI defects (key format mismatch, service role misuse) that cause silent auth failures. These are CI YAML changes only — safe to implement in parallel while Phase 3 is being planned and provisioned.

implementationPhases.name: IMPLEMENT — CI Hardening, Caching, Composite Actions, Edge Function Versioning & Backend Fixes
implementationPhases.rationale: Pin Supabase CLI, standardise codegen gate, fix bare pytest, replace JWT stubs, add job timeouts, configure npm/pip caching, implement the git-tag-triggered Edge Function versioning strategy. All CI YAML and tooling changes.

implementationPhases.name: IMPLEMENT — Supply-Chain & Runtime Security (Actions, Dependabot, Docker, Falco rules)
implementationPhases.rationale: Add GITHUB_TOKEN permission blocks, pin Actions to SHAs, add Dependabot for github-actions ecosystem, pin Docker image digests and add verify-images target. Update SoA controls 8.3 and 8.8. These harden the CI supply chain independently of the cluster.

implementationPhases.name: IMPLEMENT — Environment Tiers, GitHub Environments, Backup, Key Rotation, pgaudit & R2 Redundancy
implementationPhases.rationale: Provision staging environment, document all 4 tiers, write key management docs, configure GitHub Environments (staging + production) for secret scoping and deployment history, implement Cloudflare R2 backup (pg_dumpall/rclone) with secondary Backblaze B2 Australian region copy (ENV-11 — Australian data residency), enable pgaudit extension for database-level forensic audit logging (SEC-09), add secrets rotation schedule and reminder workflow, and configure the pull-through container registry cache. pgaudit requires Supabase (Phase 3) and Loki (Phase 3) to be running.

implementationPhases.name: PRE-FLIGHT — Phase 8 Blockers (Interval Gate)
implementationPhases.rationale: A mandatory interval gate identified during a comprehensive pre-Phase-8 review (2026-03-13). Five critical gaps and five enhancements were accepted before Phase 8 may begin. This phase contains no external infrastructure changes — all tasks are documentation, contract definitions, ADRs, and task amendments that must be committed to the audit branch before any Phase 8 CI YAML is written. The three sub-sessions for Phase 8 are also defined here as sessionGates: (8A) setup.sh + RESET; (8B) test isolation; (8C) runner migration. Phase 8 MUST NOT begin until all Phase 7.5 tasks are complete.

implementationPhases.name: IMPLEMENT — CI Architecture, Test Isolation & Branch Management
implementationPhases.rationale: Design and implement the branch-matched CI architecture (Docker network isolation or k8s namespace-per-branch), add CI mode to setup.sh, implement test data isolation and pg_cron cleanup, migrate to self-hosted runner. Reduce redundant Supabase boots (CICD-01). May be simplified by Phase 3 k8s namespace-per-branch approach. CICD-09 (composite supabase-start action) is implemented here as the output of ARCH-02 — it encapsulates the CI-native key extraction and prevents the pattern from diverging across repos again.

implementationPhases.name: IMPLEMENT — Production Gate & Compliance Finalisation
implementationPhases.rationale: Deploy the prod migration gate workflow (prod-deploy.yml) requiring human approval before any db push. Integrate the full 5-layer smoke test (CICD-10) into prod-deploy.yml and create the standalone staging-smoke.yml workflow. Update SoA control 8.32. Update Supabase governance register. CICD-10 also replaces the --ignore-health-check workaround in ephemeral CI boots with a real health gate. This phase is gated on Phase 7 (GitHub Environments must exist for environment protection rules and secret scoping).

implementationPhases.name: IMPLEMENT — Post-Provisioning Documentation (Vault Docs, Onboarding Guide)
implementationPhases.rationale: With all infrastructure running, write the post-implementation documentation: Vault configuration guide (DOC-01) documenting the OIDC setup, database secrets engine, and VSO CRD patterns; engineer onboarding guide (DOC-06) covering prerequisites, how to connect to each environment, how to run CI locally, and key contacts. These documents require a working system to be accurate.

implementationPhases.name: IMPLEMENT — Physical Security SoA Update (post-provisioning)
implementationPhases.rationale: Review ISO 27001 physical controls 7.1-7.4 post-provisioning and update the SoA with confirmed compensating controls for the k3s bare-metal node. This is a low-risk documentation task that requires the physical node to be in its final configured state before the SoA entry can be confirmed accurate.

implementationPhases.name: FINAL GATE — Branch Protection on Main (all repos)
implementationPhases.rationale: The final hardening step, enabled only once CI is stable and all phase 1-11 fixes are complete and verified. Requires the required check matrix from Phase 2 (PROC-01). Enables branch protection rulesets across all 6 repositories with mandatory CI status checks, PR approval, and no force pushes.
